# Conroe ISD Board Meeting — 2022-01-18

# Unofficial Meeting Notes
## Conroe Independent School District Board of Trustees — Regular Meeting
### January 18, 2022

*These are unofficial notes based on an auto-generated transcript and may contain errors or omissions.*

---

## Call to Order
The meeting was called to order at 6:00 PM with a quorum present. Invocation was delivered by Scott Moore; Pledge of Allegiance led by Dale Inman.

---

## Awards and Recognition

### School Board Recognition Month
- Principal Paula Krapeski of Mitchell Intermediate spoke on behalf of all CISD administrators, educators, staff, and students to recognize and thank the Board of Trustees for School Board Recognition Month.
- Principals were asked **not** to attend in person due to staffing concerns related to COVID.
- Students provided cards, posters, banners, small gifts, drawings, and treats for board members.
- Board President Hubert acknowledged the heroic efforts of teachers and substitutes, noting that over **1,100 teachers** were unable to come to work the previous Friday due to illness, with substitutes stepping in to fill the gaps.

---

## Citizen Participation (Public Comments)

**Speaker 1 (name not clearly captured):**
- Followed up on a complaint from November 16, 2021 regarding a CNN video shown in his grandson's classroom about the Pfizer COVID vaccine authorization for children ages 5–11.
- Received a phone call from Assistant Superintendent Bethany Mefford about an investigation but has not received any follow-up results.
- Sent a text message on January 2 with no response.
- Requested a follow-up call with investigation results by the following week.

**Speaker 2 (name not clearly captured):**
- A former teacher who expressed gratitude and praise for district teachers for their exhausting work during the pandemic.

---

## Superintendent's Message — Dr. Knoll
- Acknowledged the difficult staffing situation and the community-wide effort to keep schools open.
- Emphasized the district's focus on thriving, not just surviving, during the pandemic.
- Played a KHOU Channel 11 news story about 7-year-old **Andrew May**, a student with Down syndrome at **Buckalew Elementary**, whose classmates helped him make a basketball shot during recess. The story highlighted the school's culture of inclusion, kindness, and support modeled "from the top down."

---

## Consent Agenda
- No items were pulled from the consent agenda.
- **Motion to approve:** Passed unanimously by show of hands.

---

## Human Resources (Item moved up in agenda)

### Naming of Principal — Hauser Elementary
- Dr. Knoll recommended **Liz Kahler**, currently Assistant Principal at San Jacinto Elementary, as the new Principal of Hauser Elementary, replacing Angela Lozano who departed for family reasons.
- Dr. Knoll highlighted the district's investment in developing assistant principals and encouraging them to seek new opportunities at different campuses.
- **Motion to approve:** Passed unanimously by show of hands.
- Ms. Kahler delivered acceptance remarks, thanking mentors, family, and the board.

---

## Item 5: Administration

### 5A — Attendance Zone Boundary Changes for Gordon Reed Elementary
**Presented by:** Chris McCord

- **Gordon Reed Elementary** is on schedule to open August 2022 at 2045 Michaela Broad, with a capacity of **950 students**, serving **Pre-K through 6th grade** in the Conroe High feeder zone.
- The committee reviewed **27 different scenarios** over five months.
- **Recommended scenario: 7.3.3**, which impacts **557 students** (415 K–4, 142 grades 5–6) — the lowest impact among viable options.

**Key boundary changes:**
- **Elementary:** Areas north of FM 2854 transition from Giesinger to Gordon Reed; area south of FM 2854 (currently no students) transitions from B.B. Rice to Gordon Reed; remaining areas come from Stewart Elementary.
- **Intermediate:** Students feed cleanly — Stewart Elementary students go to Stewart Intermediate; Gordon Reed Elementary students go to Gordon Reed Intermediate. Some areas transition from Crier Intermediate to Gordon Reed. Sections 14B and A-15 transition from Bosman to Travis (no current students in those sections).
- **Relief provided to:** Stewart Elementary, Giesinger Elementary (projected 1,200+ students by 2028), Crier Intermediate, B.B. Rice Elementary, and Bosman Intermediate.

**Community engagement:** ~100 feedback comments received; two Zoom presentations and two in-person meetings held (one had 8–10 attendees; one had no community attendees).

**Discussion highlights:**
- Board member asked about transfer options for 5th graders at Stewart wanting to stay for 6th grade — transfers are possible with parent-provided transportation.
- Questions about economically disadvantaged (Title I) impact — no significant change anticipated; Stewart may become slightly less economically disadvantaged.
- Board President Hubert noted positive feedback from affected parents about the process.

**Vote: Passed unanimously by show of hands.**

---

### 5B — Adoption of 2022–2023 School Calendar
**Presented by:** Dr. Upshaw

- Three draft calendars were presented to the community for feedback.
- **Draft 1** received the most support from community, staff, and students.
- **Recommended calendar (Draft 1.2):**
  - **176 instructional days**
  - School starts **August 10, 2022** (one day earlier than 2021–22 start of August 11)
  - School ends **May 25, 2023** (one day earlier than current year's May 26)
  - Mid-week start continues to be popular
  - Full week off for Thanksgiving
  - Three weekends included in Christmas/winter break
  - March 13–17 Spring Break; March 20 designated as staff development day / student inclement weather day
  - **Two built-in inclement weather days**, plus the March 20 date as a potential third
  - Instructional time now calculated in minutes rather than days

**Discussion highlights:**
- Board member noted the continued trend toward earlier start dates.
- Questions about minute buffers for inclement weather — confirmed two days built in, with potential third.

**Vote: Passed unanimously by show of hands.**

---

## Public Hearing: 2020–2021 Texas Academic Performance Report (TAPR)
**Presented by:** Dr. Chris Hines

### Key highlights:
- **Accountability rating:** Not rated (state of disaster declared); met Special Education determination requirements.
- **Enrollment:** Minimal growth between 2020 and 2021; over 2,000 new students in 2021–22 (projected ~3,000 over prior year).
- **Demographics:** 84 languages other than English reported; 15,500+ families report Spanish as home language.
- **Bilingual/ESL:** District outperformed the state in many bilingual categories; bilingual scores actually increased during the pandemic year.
- **Attendance:** 99% in 2020–21 (due to remote check-in option); currently ~92% in 2021–22.
- **Disciplinary placements:** Continued 10-year downward trend in DAEP and JJAEP placements.
- **Dropout rate:** 0.3% for Class of 2020.
- **Graduation rate (Class of 2020):** 97.3% four-year graduation rate; 0.8% dropout from cohort.
- **STAAR performance:** 81% at approaches grade level; 58% meets; 32% masters.
- **College readiness:** Impacted by spring 2020 closures (missed SAT/ACT/AP tests).
- **Career/Military ready:** 15.9% of graduates.
- **SAT/ACT:** Average SAT score of 1,147; average ACT composite of 25; 64.6% scored at or above criterion.
- **Post-secondary enrollment:** 1,786 graduates from Class of 2020 enrolled in Texas public colleges; top destinations: Lone Star Montgomery, Texas A&M, Sam Houston State.
- **Financial highlights:**
  - Texas Smart School recognition for 12 consecutive years (5 stars)
  - Third consecutive TASBO Award of Merit in purchasing
  - Fund balance: $171,399,200 (29% of final amended budget)
  - Tax rate: $1.70 (among the lowest in the area)
  - Instructional expense per student: $5,433
  - General administration spending below state average
- **Criminal incidents:** 36 reportable incidents at 8 campuses; 31 of 36 were felony controlled substance offenses (majority THC vaping-related).
- **Counselors and Librarians:** 173 counselors, 65 librarians; all campuses have at least one of each.

### Public Comment on TAPR:
- **Savannah Eldridge**, Criminal Justice Chair for the local NAACP, commended the board and district for declining numbers in DAEP and JJAEP referrals and commitment to restorative practices.
- A board member requested demographic data on disciplinary placements.

---

## Item 7: Planning and Construction

### 7A — Guaranteed Maximum Price for Safety and Security 2022 Project
**Presented by:** Mr. Foster

- LS4 Constructors (selected as CMAR in February 2020) with PBK Architects and Leaf Engineers.
- **Requested amount: $5,156,997** for the 2022 installment of the district-wide safety and security project.
- Authorization for superintendent to negotiate and execute contract documents.

**Vote: Passed unanimously by show of hands.**

### 7B — Capital Improvement Updates (Information Only)
**Presented by:** Mr. Foster

| Project | Status |
|---------|--------|
| **Creighton Elementary** (overhaul) | On schedule; expanded kitchen/serving lines focus for summer; completion August 2022 |
| **Moorhead Junior High** (new, Caney Creek feeder) | Building structure essentially erected; masonry underway; on schedule for August 2023 |
| **Conroe High School** (major overhaul) | New CTE slab ready; athletic venue foundation underway; on schedule through December 2025 |
| **JET Center** (north end of Woodforest Bank Stadium) | Wrapping up; substantial completion expected late February 2022 |
| **Gordon Reed Elementary** (new) | On schedule; interior finishes underway; district occupancy early summer; open August 2022 |
| **Oak Ridge High School** (overhaul + South County CTE) | Structural steel delivered, awaiting utility clearance to go vertical; on schedule through summer 2023 |
| **Wilkerson Intermediate** (classroom addition) | Gymnasium complete and in use; cafeteria conversion complete over winter break; supply chain delays managed; basketball striping completed over recent weekend |
| **Conroe High School 9th Grade** (classroom addition) | Roof and masonry ~90% complete; interior work underway; completion expected summer 2022 |

---

## Item 8: Purchasing

### Chromebooks and Chromebook Carts (RFP 21-12-03)
**Presented by:** Mr. Reeves

- Award to **CDW-G LLC** for an estimated expenditure of **$5,000,000**.
- Purchase of approximately **14,000 Chromebooks and 100 Chromebook carts** to replace devices that can no longer update to current Google OS.
- Critical for compliance with state mandate for **online-only STAAR testing beginning December 2022**.
- 21 vendors submitted responses; pricing firm for one year with two optional one-year renewals through January 2025.
- Vendor indicates **4–8 week lead time**; purchases to be phased.
- Funded through **federal funds**.

**Vote: Passed unanimously by show of hands.**

---

## Item 9: Finance

### Approval of 2020–2021 Annual Comprehensive Financial Report (ACFR)
**Presented by:** Darren Rice, Finance; Sarah Roberts, Partner, Weaver (Independent Auditors)

**Audit results:**
- **Unmodified (clean) opinion** on financial statements — highest level of assurance
- **No reportable findings** on internal controls (no material weaknesses, no significant deficiencies)
- **Unmodified (clean) opinion** on federal award compliance with no reportable findings
- **Major programs audited:** Child Nutrition Cluster, ESSER, and Coronavirus Relief Fund (~70% coverage of total federal expenditures vs. 20% required)
- Total federal expenditures of **$82.5 million** (~$40 million above normal non-COVID year)
- General fund balance increased ~$17 million, correlated to non-recurring federal COVID funds
- Unassigned fund balance ratio at approximately **30%**
- **Note:** Report title changed from CAFR to ACFR per GASB Statement 98 (term change due to cultural sensitivity concerns)
- Auditor independence maintained; safeguards detailed regarding separate IT general controls engagement

**Board member comments:** Board member publicly thanked Mr. Rice, Ms. Garza, and the entire finance team, encouraging the public to read the annual report's detailed notes to understand district spending.

**Vote: Passed unanimously by show of hands.**

---

## Executive Session
- Closed session began at **7:28 PM** under Texas Government Code Sections 551.071 and 551.074 (consultation with attorney; personnel matters).
- Open session reconvened at **8:22 PM**.
- **No action was taken following executive session.**

---

## Adjournment
- Motion to adjourn was made and carried.
- Meeting adjourned at approximately **8:22 PM**.

---
